one_line: Two Hindustan Republican Association revolutionaries of Kakori, united in ideals and sacrifice in 1920s United Provinces.

appearance.age_look: Mid-20s, lean but wiry builds; Ashfaq slightly taller and narrower, Bismil a touch broader at shoulders.
appearance.face: Ashfaq: oval face, wheatish complexion, neatly trimmed narrow moustache, calm intense eyes; Bismil: slightly squarer face, fair-to-wheatish, fuller straight moustache, sharper gaze; both default to a determined, quietly resolute expression.
appearance.hair: Ashfaq: short, neatly combed black hair with a side part, usually under a plain white Gandhi cap; Bismil: short black hair brushed back, often bare-headed or with a folded off-white angavastram over one side when outdoors.
appearance.costume: Ashfaq wears a simple 1920s khadi outfit: a plain off-white long kurta (knee-length, straight cut, minimal collar), loose off-white churidar pyjamas, and a sleeveless light-brown woollen waistcoat in colder scenes, with a plain white Gandhi cap and worn brown leather sandals; Bismil wears a slightly more formal Congress-era style: a crisp off-white khadi kurta (knee-length with a small band collar), straight white pyjamas, a light-grey cotton Nehru-style waistcoat with three buttons, sometimes a thin dark-brown leather belt visible at the waist, an off-white angavastram/dupatta loosely draped over one shoulder, and simple black leather lace-up shoes.
appearance.signature: A single well-used brown leather shoulder satchel they share between them, bulging slightly with folded papers and a cloth-bound notebook, strap crossing the chest; Ashfaq often grips the strap firmly while Bismil sometimes holds a visible corner of a handwritten notebook emerging from it.

ref_desc: Ashfaq is a lean mid‑20s man with an oval wheatish face, narrow moustache, short side‑parted black hair under a white Gandhi cap, in off‑white kurta‑pyjama with brown waistcoat and sandals, beside Bismil, a slightly broader fair‑wheatish man with a squarer face, straight moustache, short brushed‑back hair, in off‑white kurta‑pyjama, light‑grey waistcoat, off‑white angavastram and black shoes, and they share a worn brown leather shoulder satchel of papers in an off‑white, light‑brown, dark‑brown, light‑grey and black palette.
sheet_prompt: Draw two full‑body male characters from 1920s North India in anime style, standing side by side as consistent model designs: Ashfaq is a lean mid‑20s man with an oval wheatish face, calm intense eyes, a neat narrow moustache, and short side‑parted black hair mostly covered by a plain white Gandhi cap; he wears a simple off‑white khadi long kurta reaching just above the knees with a minimal band collar, loose off‑white churidar pyjamas gathered at the ankles, and a sleeveless light‑brown woollen waistcoat with three simple buttons over the kurta in some poses, along with worn brown leather open sandals, his posture slightly relaxed but his hand gripping the strap of a single brown leather shoulder satchel crossing his chest. Beside him Bismil is a slightly broader‑shouldered mid‑20s man with a squarer fair‑to‑wheatish face, sharper determined eyes, and a fuller straight moustache, with short black hair brushed back and usually uncovered; he wears a crisp off‑white khadi kurta of similar length with a small band collar, straight white pyjamas, a light‑grey cotton Nehru‑style waistcoat with three visible buttons, a slim dark‑brown leather belt hinted at under the waistcoat, an off‑white angavastram or dupatta loosely draped over one shoulder, and simple black leather lace‑up shoes. Show the worn brown leather satchel as their shared signature prop, bulging slightly with folded papers and a cloth‑bound notebook, sometimes in Ashfaq’s grip on the strap and sometimes with Bismil touching the exposed notebook corner; keep the colour palette dominated by off‑white garments with light‑brown, dark‑brown, light‑grey and black accents, and present them in a neutral stance with clear head‑to‑toe views to serve as a consistent character sheet.
